name: Millet Cakes with Carrot Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and drain the millet.
2. Bring water and salt to a boil in a saucepan. Stir in the millet and return to a boil.
3. Lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes or until the water is absorbed, stirring occasionally.
4. Press the cooked millet firmly into a 9-inch by 5-inch by 3-inch bread pan.
5. Let the cakes cool and cut into half-inch wide slices. Set aside.
6. Steam carrots until soft, reserving cooking water.
7. Puree the steamed carrots and tofu together, adding enough reserved cooking water to achieve a smooth sauce consistency.
8. Blend tahini, miso, and umeboshi vinegar into the carrot-tofu puree.
9. Heat through the mixture, stirring constantly.
10. Remove from heat and set aside.
11. Cut wide strips of nori long enough to wrap around each millet cake.
12. Heat a skillet and brush generously with corn oil. Fry millet cakes for one minute on each side until golden brown.
13. Wrap each fried millet cake with a strip of nori.
14. Top with carrot sauce and serve.
